title: <div>  •  <b>McKee’s principles (conflict &amp escalation) still apply—but they
content:
• McKee’s principles (conflict & escalation) still apply—but they must be translated into pacing and layout.
• If your protagonist hesitates before making a choice, the hesitation should be felt in the panel composition (wider spaces, pauses in dialogue, silent panels).

2. Conflict vs. Closure: What Drives Reader Engagement?

McKee: Conflict is the Engine of Storytelling

“True character is revealed in the choices a human being makes under pressure—the greater the pressure, the deeper the revelation.”

🔥 Example from Whiplash
• Andrew’s arc is driven by his escalating clashes with Fletcher—each one revealing new aspects of his personality.

McKee argues that tension keeps an audience engaged. Without it, the story stalls.

McCloud: Closure is the Engine of Comics

“Closure is the phenomenon of observing the parts but perceiving the whole.”
context: From document: 1.1.3. | Narrative Transcends Medium | Subheading — chunk 3 (chunk 3/7)
